oxygen.en: a colorless gas with no smell that people, animals, and plants need in order to live

ex.en: We need oxygen to breathe.
ex.ko: 우리는 숨을 쉬기 위해 산소가 필요하다.

pace.en: walk back and forth in a small space, often because you are anxious

ex.en: She paced back and forth in the waiting room.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대기실에서 왔다갔다 서성거렸다.

pace.en: the speed at which something happens or moves

ex.en: The pace of change in technology is incredible.
ex.ko: 기술 변화의 속도는 놀랍다.

package.en: a box or container in which something is wrapped or packed, especially to be sent by mail

ex.en: A package arrived at my door this morning.
ex.ko: 오늘 아침 소포가 문 앞에 도착했다.

package.en: a set of related things, such as products or services, offered or sold together

ex.en: The hotel offers a package including breakfast and airport transfer.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 조식과 공항 이동을 포함한 패키지를 제공한다.

packing.en: the act of putting your things into a bag or box before a trip

ex.en: I always leave packing until the last minute.
ex.ko: 나는 항상 짐 싸는 것을 마지막 순간까지 미룬다.

packing.en: material used to protect something inside a box during shipping

ex.en: The glasses were wrapped in plenty of packing to prevent breakage.
ex.ko: 그 유리잔들은 깨지지 않도록 완충재로 잘 포장되어 있었다.

paddle.en: a short pole with a wide flat blade at one end, used to move a small boat through water

ex.en: He used a paddle to move the canoe across the lake.
ex.ko: 그는 패들을 사용해 호수를 가로질러 카누를 저었다.

paddle.en: move a small boat through water using a paddle

ex.en: They paddled slowly down the river.
ex.ko: 그들은 강을 따라 천천히 노를 저어 내려갔다.

paddle.en: walk or play in shallow water for fun

ex.en: The children paddled happily at the edge of the sea.
ex.ko: 아이들은 바닷가 얕은 물에서 신나게 물장구쳤다.

ex.en: She has a paid internship this summer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이번 여름에 유급 인턴십을 한다.

paid.en: (of a bill or amount) already paid

ex.en: All the bills for this month are paid.
ex.ko: 이번 달 청구서는 모두 납부되었다.

pain.en: an uncomfortable feeling in your body caused by illness or injury

ex.en: She felt a sharp pain in her knee.
ex.ko: 그녀는 무릎에서 날카로운 통증을 느꼈다.

pain.en: strong feelings of unhappiness or emotional suffering

ex.en: Losing her job caused her a lot of pain.
ex.ko: 직장을 잃은 것은 그녀에게 큰 고통을 주었다.

ex.en: My little brother can be such a pain sometimes.
ex.ko: 내 남동생은 가끔 정말 골칫거리다.

ex.en: My back is still painful after the fall.
ex.ko: 넘어진 후 내 등이 아직도 아프다.

painful.en: causing emotional distress or difficulty to think about

ex.en: Talking about the accident is still painful for her.
ex.ko: 그 사고에 대해 이야기하는 것은 그녀에게 여전히 괴로운 일이다.

pale.en: (of a person's face) having less color than usual, often because of illness or shock

ex.en: He looked pale after hearing the news.
ex.ko: 그는 그 소식을 듣고 창백해 보였다.

pale.en: light in color, not dark or bright

ex.en: She wore a pale blue dress.
ex.ko: 그녀는 연한 파란색 드레스를 입었다.

palm.en: the inner surface of your hand between your wrist and fingers

ex.en: She held the coin in the palm of her hand.
ex.ko: 그녀는 동전을 손바닥에 쥐고 있었다.

palm.en: a tropical tree with large leaves at the top and no branches

ex.en: Palm trees lined the beach.
ex.ko: 야자수들이 해변을 따라 늘어서 있었다.

parachute.en: a large piece of cloth that opens up in the air to let a person or object fall to the ground slowly and safely

ex.en: He jumped out of the plane and opened his parachute.
ex.ko: 그는 비행기에서 뛰어내려 낙하산을 펼쳤다.

paradise.en: a place or situation that is extremely beautiful or that makes you extremely happy

ex.en: This beach is like paradise.
ex.ko: 이 해변은 낙원 같다.

paradise.cue: 죽은 후 착한 사람이 가는 곳 (기독교)
paradise.en: (in Christian belief) the place where good people go after death

ex.en: They believe the soul goes to paradise after death.
ex.ko: 그들은 영혼이 죽은 후 천국에 간다고 믿는다.

paralyze.en: make a person or part of the body unable to move or feel anything

ex.en: The accident paralyzed him from the waist down.
ex.ko: 그 사고로 그는 허리 아래가 마비되었다.

paralyze.en: make a system, organization, or activity unable to work or continue normally

ex.en: The heavy snow paralyzed the entire city.
ex.ko: 폭설이 도시 전체를 마비시켰다.

parcel.en: something wrapped in paper or a box, especially to be sent by mail

ex.en: The mailman delivered a parcel to our house.
ex.ko: 우편배달부가 우리 집에 소포를 배달했다.

parcel.en: a piece of land, especially one that is part of a larger area

ex.en: The company bought a parcel of land near the river.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 강 근처의 땅 한 필지를 매입했다.

parental.en: relating to being a parent or to parents

ex.en: Children need parental guidance and support.
ex.ko: 아이들은 부모의 지도와 지지가 필요하다.

parking.en: the act of leaving a vehicle somewhere for a period of time

ex.en: Parking is difficult to find downtown.
ex.ko: 시내에서는 주차할 곳을 찾기 어렵다.

parking.en: a space or area where vehicles can be parked

ex.en: The building has free parking for visitors.
ex.ko: 그 건물은 방문객을 위한 무료 주차 공간이 있다.

parrot.en: a brightly colored tropical bird that can copy human speech

ex.en: The parrot can say a few simple words.
ex.ko: 그 앵무새는 간단한 단어 몇 개를 말할 수 있다.

parrot.cue: 이해 없이 그대로 따라 말하는 사람
parrot.en: a person who repeats what someone else says without really understanding it

ex.en: Don't just be a parrot — think for yourself.
ex.ko: 그냥 앵무새처럼 따라 하지 말고 스스로 생각해.

part-time.en: working or done for less than the usual full number of hours

ex.en: She has a part-time job at a cafe.
ex.ko: 그녀는 카페에서 파트타임으로 일한다.

part-time.en: for less than the usual full number of working hours

ex.en: He works part-time while studying.
ex.ko: 그는 공부하면서 파트타임으로 일한다.

partial.en: affecting or including only a part, not the whole

ex.en: The company issued a partial refund.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 부분 환불을 해주었다.

partial.en: unfairly favoring one person or side over another

ex.en: The referee was accused of being partial to the home team.
ex.ko: 그 심판은 홈팀에 편파적이라는 비난을 받았다.

participant.en: a person who takes part in an activity or event

ex.en: All participants received a certificate at the end of the workshop.
ex.ko: 모든 참가자는 워크숍이 끝날 때 수료증을 받았다.

participate.en: take part in an activity or event together with other people

ex.en: Over a hundred students participated in the competition.
ex.ko: 백 명이 넘는 학생들이 그 대회에 참가했다.

participle.en: a form of a verb, such as the -ing or -ed form, used to make certain tenses or as an adjective

ex.en: "Running" is the present participle of "run".
ex.ko: 'running'은 'run'의 현재분사이다.

particularly.en: more than usual or more than other things

ex.en: I particularly enjoyed the last chapter of the book.
ex.ko: 나는 그 책의 마지막 장이 특히 좋았다.

passion.cue: 어떤 일에 대한 강한 애정과 열의
passion.en: a very strong feeling of enthusiasm or love for something

ex.en: She has a real passion for painting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그림에 대한 진정한 열정을 가지고 있다.

passion.en: something that you like or enjoy very much

ex.en: Cooking has always been his passion.
ex.ko: 요리는 항상 그의 열정의 대상이었다.

passive.en: not doing anything to react to or change a situation; accepting what happens

ex.en: He took a passive role in the discussion.
ex.ko: 그는 토론에서 수동적인 역할을 맡았다.

passive.en: a grammatical form in which the subject receives the action of the verb, rather than performing it

ex.en: "The cake was eaten" is an example of the passive voice.
ex.ko: "The cake was eaten"는 수동태의 예시이다.

passport.cue: 해외로 나갈 때 신분을 증명하는 문서
passport.en: an official document issued by a government that lets you travel to other countries and shows who you are

ex.en: Don't forget to bring your passport to the airport.
ex.ko: 공항에 여권 가져오는 것 잊지 마.

password.en: a secret word or set of characters that you must type in to be allowed to use a computer system or account

ex.en: Please choose a strong password for your account.
ex.ko: 계정에 사용할 강력한 비밀번호를 선택하세요.

patience.en: the ability to stay calm and wait for a long time or deal with difficulties without complaining

ex.en: Teaching young children requires a lot of patience.
ex.ko: 어린아이들을 가르치는 데는 많은 인내심이 필요하다.

patrol.en: the act of regularly walking or traveling around an area to make sure it is safe

ex.en: Police officers patrol this neighborhood every night.
ex.ko: 경찰관들이 매일 밤 이 동네를 순찰한다.

pattern.en: a repeated arrangement of lines, shapes, or colors as a design

ex.en: She chose fabric with a floral pattern.
ex.ko: 그녀는 꽃무늬가 있는 천을 골랐다.

pattern.en: the way in which something regularly happens or is done

ex.en: Scientists studied the weather patterns of the region.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 그 지역의 날씨 패턴을 연구했다.

pause.en: a short stop in speaking or doing something before continuing

ex.en: There was a long pause before she answered.
ex.ko: 그녀가 대답하기 전에 긴 정적이 흘렀다.

pause.en: stop doing something for a short time before starting again

ex.en: He paused the movie to answer the phone.
ex.ko: 그는 전화를 받으려고 영화를 잠시 멈췄다.

peanut.en: a small nut that grows underground and is often eaten roasted or salted

ex.en: She is allergic to peanuts.
ex.ko: 그녀는 땅콩 알레르기가 있다.

peculiar.en: strange or unusual, in a way that seems odd

ex.en: There was a peculiar smell coming from the kitchen.
ex.ko: 부엌에서 이상한 냄새가 났다.

peculiar.en: belonging only to a particular person, place, or thing

ex.en: This custom is peculiar to a few villages in the region.
ex.ko: 이 풍습은 그 지역의 몇몇 마을에만 특유한 것이다.

ex.en: He arrived in the city penniless and alone.
ex.ko: 그는 무일푼으로 혼자 그 도시에 도착했다.

penny.en: a small coin worth one hundredth of a pound or dollar

ex.en: I found a penny on the sidewalk.
ex.ko: 나는 인도에서 페니 동전 하나를 발견했다.

ex.en: The room costs 100 dollars per night.
ex.ko: 그 방은 하룻밤에 100달러이다.

percent.en: a part or share of a whole expressed as a number out of 100

ex.en: Only ten percent of the students failed the test.
ex.ko: 학생들의 단 10퍼센트만이 시험에 떨어졌다.

performer.en: a person who performs for an audience, such as an actor, singer, or musician

ex.en: The street performer drew a large crowd.
ex.ko: 그 거리 공연자는 많은 관중을 끌어모았다.

permanent.en: continuing to exist or stay the same forever, without any planned change

ex.en: The injury caused permanent damage to his knee.
ex.ko: 그 부상은 그의 무릎에 영구적인 손상을 입혔다.

permanently.en: in a way that will continue forever, without change

ex.en: The factory closed permanently last year.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 작년에 영구적으로 문을 닫았다.

permit.en: allow something to happen or someone to do something

ex.en: Smoking is not permitted inside the building.
ex.ko: 건물 내에서는 흡연이 허용되지 않는다.

permit.en: an official document that gives you permission to do something

ex.en: You need a permit to park here.
ex.ko: 여기에 주차하려면 허가증이 필요하다.

personally.en: used to show that you are giving your own opinion

ex.en: Personally, I don't think that's a good idea.
ex.ko: 개인적으로 나는 그게 좋은 생각이라고 생각하지 않는다.

personally.en: done by yourself rather than through someone else

ex.en: The manager personally handled the complaint.
ex.ko: 그 매니저가 직접 그 불만을 처리했다.

persuade.en: make someone decide to do something by giving them good reasons

ex.en: She persuaded her parents to let her travel alone.
ex.ko: 그녀는 부모님을 설득해서 혼자 여행할 수 있게 했다.

persuasive.en: able to make people believe or do something

ex.en: He gave a persuasive speech about climate change.
ex.ko: 그는 기후 변화에 관한 설득력 있는 연설을 했다.

phantom.cue: 눈에 보이는 듯하지만 실체가 없는 유령
phantom.en: a ghost, or something that seems to exist but is not real

ex.en: He claimed to have seen a phantom in the old house.
ex.ko: 그는 그 낡은 집에서 유령을 봤다고 주장했다.

pharmacy.en: a shop where medicines are sold or given out

ex.en: I need to stop by the pharmacy to pick up my prescription.
ex.ko: 처방약을 받으러 약국에 들러야 한다.

phenomenon.en: a fact or event, especially one that is unusual or difficult to understand

ex.en: Global warming is a phenomenon affecting the whole planet.
ex.ko: 지구온난화는 지구 전체에 영향을 미치는 현상이다.

phenomenon.en: someone or something that is remarkably talented or successful

ex.en: The young singer is a real phenomenon in the music industry.
ex.ko: 그 어린 가수는 음악 업계에서 진짜 경이로운 존재이다.

philosopher.cue: 삶과 존재에 대해 깊이 사유하는 사람
philosopher.en: a person who studies or writes about fundamental questions of life, knowledge, and existence

ex.en: Socrates is one of the most famous philosophers in history.
ex.ko: 소크라테스는 역사상 가장 유명한 철학자 중 한 명이다.

philosophy.en: the study of fundamental questions about existence, knowledge, and how people should live

ex.en: She majored in philosophy at university.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대학에서 철학을 전공했다.

philosophy.cue: 어떤 사람이 삶을 대하는 개인적인 신념·태도
philosophy.en: a personal set of beliefs about how to live or deal with situations

ex.en: His philosophy is to always look on the bright side.
ex.ko: 그의 철학은 항상 밝은 면을 보는 것이다.

phoenix.cue: 불에 타 죽었다가 재에서 다시 태어난다는 전설의 새
phoenix.en: a legendary bird that is said to burn to death and then rise again from its ashes

ex.en: Like a phoenix, the company rose again from near bankruptcy.
ex.ko: 불사조처럼, 그 회사는 파산 직전에서 다시 일어섰다.

photocopy.en: a copy of a document made using a machine

ex.en: Could you make a photocopy of this page for me?
ex.ko: 이 페이지 복사본 하나 만들어 줄 수 있어?

photocopy.en: make a copy of a document using a machine

ex.en: I need to photocopy my ID before the interview.
ex.ko: 면접 전에 내 신분증을 복사해야 한다.

phrasal verb.cue: 동사+전치사/부사가 합쳐져 새 뜻을 만드는 표현
phrasal verb.en: a verb combined with a preposition or adverb that together have a meaning different from the individual words

ex.en: "Give up" is a common phrasal verb meaning to stop trying.
ex.ko: 'give up'은 포기한다는 뜻의 흔한 구동사이다.

phrase.en: a group of words that together form a unit of meaning, but not a complete sentence

ex.en: Try to memorize this useful phrase for your trip.
ex.ko: 여행을 위해 이 유용한 문구를 외워 봐.

physics.en: the scientific study of matter, energy, and how they interact

ex.en: He is studying physics at university.
ex.ko: 그는 대학에서 물리학을 공부하고 있다.

pie.en: a food made of pastry filled with fruit, meat, or vegetables and baked

ex.en: She baked an apple pie for dessert.
ex.ko: 그녀는 후식으로 애플파이를 구웠다.

piety.en: deep respect for God or religion, shown through your beliefs and actions

ex.en: The old monk was known for his piety.
ex.ko: 그 늙은 수도승은 신앙심으로 유명했다.

pillow.en: a soft cushion that you rest your head on when sleeping

ex.en: He fluffed up his pillow before going to sleep.
ex.ko: 그는 자기 전에 베개를 통통하게 두드려 폈다.

pin.cue: 천이나 종이를 고정하는 뾰족한 작은 물건
pin.en: a small, thin, sharp piece of metal used to fasten things together

ex.en: She used a pin to attach the note to the board.
ex.ko: 그녀는 핀을 사용해 게시판에 쪽지를 고정했다.

pin.en: fasten something in place using a pin or by holding it firmly so it cannot move

ex.en: He was pinned to the ground during the wrestling match.
ex.ko: 그는 레슬링 경기에서 바닥에 고정된 채 눌려 있었다.

pin.en: a secret number you use to access your bank account or card

ex.en: Please enter your PIN to complete the payment.
ex.ko: 결제를 완료하려면 비밀번호를 입력해 주세요.

pineapple.cue: 뾰족한 잎이 달린 노란 열대 과일
pineapple.en: a large tropical fruit with sweet yellow flesh and spiky leaves on top

ex.en: She cut the pineapple into small pieces.
ex.ko: 그녀는 파인애플을 작게 잘랐다.

pipe.en: a long tube made of metal or plastic used to carry water, oil, or gas

ex.en: A water pipe burst under the street.
ex.ko: 길 아래에서 수도관이 터졌다.

pipe.cue: 담배를 피우는 작은 대롱 모양 도구
pipe.en: a small tube with a bowl at one end, used for smoking tobacco

ex.en: The old man smoked a pipe on the porch every evening.
ex.ko: 그 노인은 매일 저녁 현관에서 파이프 담배를 피웠다.

pit.en: a large hole dug in the ground

ex.en: Workers dug a pit to bury the pipeline.
ex.ko: 작업자들은 배관을 묻기 위해 구덩이를 팠다.

pit.en: the hard seed found in the middle of some fruits, such as peaches or cherries

ex.en: Be careful not to bite the pit inside the peach.
ex.ko: 복숭아 속 씨를 씹지 않도록 조심해.

pit.en: an area at the side of a racetrack where race cars are serviced during a race

ex.en: The driver stopped at the pit to change tires.
ex.ko: 그 운전자는 타이어를 교체하려고 피트에 멈췄다.

plain.en: simple in style, without decoration or a pattern

ex.en: She wore a plain white T-shirt.
ex.ko: 그녀는 무늬 없는 흰색 티셔츠를 입었다.

plain.en: easy to see, hear, or understand; obvious

ex.en: It was plain that he didn't want to talk about it.
ex.ko: 그가 그 얘기를 하고 싶어하지 않는다는 것이 분명했다.

plain.cue: 나무나 산이 없는 넓고 평평한 땅
plain.en: a large area of flat land with few trees

ex.en: Herds of animals roamed across the plain.
ex.ko: 동물 무리들이 평원을 가로질러 돌아다녔다.

platform.en: a raised, flat area where passengers wait for and get on a train

ex.en: The train departs from platform 4.
ex.ko: 그 기차는 4번 승강장에서 출발한다.

platform.en: a computer system or online service that other programs or services are built on or run through

ex.en: The company launched a new video streaming platform.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 새로운 동영상 스트리밍 플랫폼을 출시했다.

pleasantly.en: in a way that is enjoyable or makes you feel good

ex.en: I was pleasantly surprised by how well the trip went.
ex.ko: 여행이 잘 진행되어서 기분 좋게 놀랐다.

plug.en: a device at the end of an electrical cord that you put into a socket to connect power

ex.en: Make sure the plug is fully inserted into the socket.
ex.ko: 플러그가 콘센트에 완전히 꽂혔는지 확인해.

plug.en: connect an electrical device to a power supply by putting its plug into a socket

ex.en: Plug the charger in before you go to bed.
ex.ko: 자기 전에 충전기 플러그를 꽂아 놔.

plug.en: fill a hole or gap tightly to close it

ex.en: He plugged the hole in the boat with some cloth.
ex.ko: 그는 천으로 배에 난 구멍을 막았다.

pocket money.cue: 부모가 아이에게 정기적으로 주는 소액의 돈
pocket money.en: a small amount of money given regularly to a child by parents for personal spending

ex.en: He saves half of his pocket money every week.
ex.ko: 그는 매주 용돈의 절반을 저축한다.

ex.en: She is a well-known poet in her country.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자기 나라에서 유명한 시인이다.

poetry.en: writing arranged with a rhythm, often using rhyme, that expresses feelings and ideas

ex.en: She reads poetry every night before bed.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매일 밤 자기 전에 시를 읽는다.

poison.en: a substance that can cause illness or death if it enters the body

ex.en: The chemical is a poison if swallowed.
ex.ko: 그 화학 물질은 삼키면 독이 된다.

poison.en: add a harmful substance to food or drink, or make someone sick or die by giving them poison

ex.en: The villain tried to poison the king's wine.
ex.ko: 그 악당은 왕의 포도주에 독을 넣으려 했다.

poisonous.en: containing poison, or capable of causing illness or death

ex.en: Some mushrooms in the forest are poisonous.
ex.ko: 숲속의 일부 버섯은 독이 있다.

pole.en: a long, thin piece of wood or metal, usually round

ex.en: They put up a flag on a tall pole.
ex.ko: 그들은 긴 장대에 깃발을 달았다.

pole.en: either of the two points at the top and bottom of the Earth's axis

ex.en: Very few animals can survive at the North Pole.
ex.ko: 북극에서 살아남을 수 있는 동물은 거의 없다.

policy.en: an official plan of action agreed on by a government, business, or other group

ex.en: The government announced a new policy on education.
ex.ko: 정부는 교육에 관한 새로운 정책을 발표했다.

policy.en: a written agreement with an insurance company

ex.en: He checked his insurance policy before the surgery.
ex.ko: 그는 수술 전에 보험 증권을 확인했다.

politely.en: in a way that shows good manners and respect for others

ex.en: She politely asked the waiter for the bill.
ex.ko: 그녀는 웨이터에게 정중하게 계산서를 요청했다.

politician.en: a person who works in government or is elected to public office

ex.en: The politician promised to lower taxes.
ex.ko: 그 정치인은 세금을 낮추겠다고 약속했다.

politics.en: the activities of the government and people who try to influence how a country is run

ex.en: He has never been interested in politics.
ex.ko: 그는 정치에 전혀 관심이 없었다.

pollutant.en: a substance that makes air, water, or soil dirty or harmful

ex.en: Factories release pollutants into the river.
ex.ko: 공장들은 강에 오염물질을 배출한다.

pond.en: a small area of still water, smaller than a lake

ex.en: Ducks were swimming in the pond.
ex.ko: 오리들이 연못에서 헤엄치고 있었다.

popcorn.cue: 열을 가하면 부풀어 터지는 옥수수 간식
popcorn.en: a snack made from corn kernels that are heated until they burst open and puff up

ex.en: We always eat popcorn while watching a movie.
ex.ko: 우리는 영화를 볼 때 항상 팝콘을 먹는다.

pork.en: meat that comes from a pig

ex.en: She grilled some pork for dinner.
ex.ko: 그녀는 저녁으로 돼지고기를 구웠다.

port.cue: 배가 드나들며 짐을 싣고 내리는 곳
port.en: a town or area by the sea or a river where ships load and unload goods or passengers

ex.en: The ship arrived at the port early in the morning.
ex.ko: 그 배는 이른 아침에 항구에 도착했다.

port.en: a place on a computer where you can connect another device with a cable

ex.en: Plug the mouse into the USB port.
ex.ko: 마우스를 USB 포트에 꽂아.

positive.en: feeling or showing hope and confidence that things will turn out well

ex.en: Try to stay positive during difficult times.
ex.ko: 힘든 시기에도 긍정적으로 지내려고 노력해.

positive.en: completely sure that something is true

ex.en: Are you positive you locked the door?
ex.ko: 문을 잠갔다고 확신해?

positive.en: showing that a particular substance or condition is present, as in a medical test

ex.en: His test came back positive for the virus.
ex.ko: 그의 검사 결과는 그 바이러스에 양성으로 나왔다.

positively.en: in a hopeful or favorable way

ex.en: He responded positively to the new plan.
ex.ko: 그는 새 계획에 긍정적으로 반응했다.

positively.en: used to emphasize that something is definitely true

ex.en: That was positively the best meal I've ever had.
ex.ko: 그건 정말로 내가 먹어본 최고의 식사였다.

ex.en: He possesses a large collection of rare books.
ex.ko: 그는 희귀한 책들을 많이 소유하고 있다.

possess.en: have a particular quality, ability, or characteristic

ex.en: She possesses great patience with children.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아이들에게 대단한 인내심을 지니고 있다.

possession.en: something that you own or have with you

ex.en: He packed all his possessions into one bag.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 모든 소지품을 가방 하나에 챙겼다.

possession.en: the state of owning or having something

ex.en: The stolen goods were found in his possession.
ex.ko: 도난품이 그의 소유물 속에서 발견되었다.

possessive.en: wanting to keep someone or something only for yourself, and not liking to share

ex.en: He gets possessive whenever his girlfriend talks to other guys.
ex.ko: 그는 여자친구가 다른 남자들과 얘기하면 소유욕이 강해진다.

possessive.en: (in grammar) showing that something belongs to someone

ex.en: "My" and "her" are possessive adjectives.
ex.ko: 'my'와 'her'는 소유격 형용사이다.

possibility.en: a chance that something might happen or be true

ex.en: There is a possibility that the flight will be delayed.
ex.ko: 그 비행기가 지연될 가능성이 있다.

postcard.cue: 여행지에서 사진과 함께 보내는 우편 카드
postcard.en: a card, often with a picture on one side, that you send by mail without an envelope

ex.en: She sent us a postcard from Paris.
ex.ko: 그녀는 파리에서 우리에게 엽서를 보냈다.

postman.en: a person whose job is to deliver letters and packages

ex.en: The postman delivers our mail every morning.
ex.ko: 우편배달부는 매일 아침 우리 우편물을 배달한다.

pot.cue: 요리할 때 쓰는 깊고 둥근 그릇
pot.en: a round, deep container used for cooking

ex.en: She put the pot of soup on the stove.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수프 냄비를 가스레인지 위에 올렸다.

pot.en: a container used for growing a plant in

ex.en: He planted the seeds in a small pot.
ex.ko: 그는 작은 화분에 씨앗을 심었다.

potential.en: capable of happening or being developed in the future, though not yet real

ex.en: They discussed several potential solutions to the problem.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 문제에 대한 여러 잠재적 해결책을 논의했다.

potential.en: the ability someone or something has to develop or succeed in the future

ex.en: The coach believes in the team's potential.
ex.ko: 그 코치는 팀의 잠재력을 믿는다.

pottery.en: objects such as pots and bowls made from clay that has been shaped and baked

ex.en: The museum displays ancient pottery from the region.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 그 지역의 고대 도자기를 전시한다.

pound.en: the unit of money used in the United Kingdom

ex.en: The book costs ten pounds.
ex.ko: 그 책은 10파운드이다.

pound.en: a unit of weight equal to about 0.45 kilograms

ex.en: The baby weighed seven pounds at birth.
ex.ko: 그 아기는 태어났을 때 7파운드였다.

ex.en: He pounded on the door until someone answered.
ex.ko: 그는 누군가 대답할 때까지 문을 세게 두드렸다.

poverty.en: the state of being extremely poor, without enough money for basic needs

ex.en: Millions of people around the world live in poverty.
ex.ko: 전 세계 수백만 명의 사람들이 빈곤 속에 살고 있다.

powder.en: a dry substance made of very fine particles

ex.en: Mix the powder with water to make the paint.
ex.ko: 물감을 만들려면 가루를 물과 섞어.

powder.en: crush something into very small pieces or dust

ex.en: The spices are powdered before being packaged.
ex.ko: 그 향신료는 포장되기 전에 가루로 만들어진다.

practical.en: suitable for the real, actual use of something rather than just theory

ex.en: She gave us some practical advice on saving money.
ex.ko: 그녀는 돈을 절약하는 것에 대한 실용적인 조언을 해주었다.

praise.cue: 잘한 일에 대해 좋은 말을 해주다
praise.en: express approval or admiration for someone or something

ex.en: The teacher praised her for the excellent essay.
ex.ko: 선생님은 훌륭한 에세이에 대해 그녀를 칭찬했다.

praise.en: words that show approval or admiration

ex.en: He received a lot of praise for his performance.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 공연으로 많은 칭찬을 받았다.

prayer.en: words spoken to God, especially to give thanks or ask for help

ex.en: She said a prayer before the meal.
ex.ko: 그녀는 식사 전에 기도를 했다.

precious.en: of great value, either in money or because it is important to you

ex.en: This necklace is a precious gift from my grandmother.
ex.ko: 이 목걸이는 할머니께서 주신 소중한 선물이다.

preference.cue: 여러 개 중 더 좋아하는 것
preference.en: a liking for one thing more than another

ex.en: What is your preference, tea or coffee?
ex.ko: 차와 커피 중 뭘 더 선호해?

prefix.en: a group of letters added to the beginning of a word that changes its meaning

ex.en: The prefix "un-" means "not", as in "unhappy".
ex.ko: 접두사 'un-'은 'unhappy'처럼 '~하지 않은'을 뜻한다.

pregnant.en: (of a woman) having a baby developing inside her body

ex.en: She is seven months pregnant.
ex.ko: 그녀는 임신 7개월이다.

prejudice.cue: 충분히 알지 못하면서 미리 갖는 부정적 생각
prejudice.en: an unfair and unreasonable opinion or feeling about a person or group, formed without enough knowledge

ex.en: The company was accused of racial prejudice in hiring.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 채용에서 인종 편견이 있다는 비난을 받았다.

preparation.en: the act of getting ready for something that will happen

ex.en: The preparation for the wedding took months.
ex.ko: 결혼식 준비는 몇 달이 걸렸다.

prepared.en: having made the necessary arrangements for something in advance

ex.en: Make sure you are prepared for the exam.
ex.ko: 시험 준비가 되어 있는지 확인해.

preposition.cue: 명사·대명사 앞에서 위치·시간 등을 나타내는 문법 요소
preposition.en: a word such as "in", "on", or "to" that comes before a noun or pronoun to show place, time, or direction

ex.en: "On" is a preposition in the sentence "The book is on the table."
ex.ko: "The book is on the table."에서 'on'은 전치사이다.

preschool.cue: 초등학교에 들어가기 전 어린이를 위한 교육 기관
preschool.en: a school for young children who are not yet old enough for elementary school

ex.en: My daughter starts preschool next month.
ex.ko: 내 딸은 다음 달에 유치원에 다니기 시작한다.

prescription.en: a written instruction from a doctor that allows you to get a particular medicine

ex.en: The doctor gave her a prescription for antibiotics.
ex.ko: 의사는 그녀에게 항생제 처방전을 써주었다.

presence.en: the fact of being in a particular place

ex.en: Her presence made everyone feel calmer.
ex.ko: 그녀가 있다는 것만으로 모두가 더 편안해졌다.

presence.en: the state of being at a particular event or place

ex.en: Your presence at the meeting is required.
ex.ko: 회의에 참석하는 것이 필수이다.

presentation.en: a talk in which you explain information or ideas to a group of people

ex.en: She gave a presentation on climate change.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기후 변화에 관한 발표를 했다.

preservation.cue: 오래된 것·자연을 잘 보호해서 남기는 일
preservation.en: the act of keeping something safe from damage, loss, or change

ex.en: The park was created for the preservation of wildlife.
ex.ko: 그 공원은 야생 동물 보존을 위해 만들어졌다.

preserve.en: keep something in its original state, protecting it from damage or change

ex.en: They work to preserve the old castle.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 오래된 성을 보존하기 위해 노력한다.

preserve.en: treat food so that it does not go bad for a long time

ex.en: Salt has long been used to preserve meat.
ex.ko: 소금은 오래전부터 고기를 보존하는 데 사용되어 왔다.

president.en: the leader of a country that does not have a king or queen

ex.en: The president gave a speech to the nation.
ex.ko: 대통령은 국민들에게 연설을 했다.

president.en: the person in charge of a company or organization

ex.en: She was elected president of the student council.
ex.ko: 그녀는 학생회 회장으로 선출되었다.

press.en: push something firmly with your finger or hand

ex.en: Press the button to start the machine.
ex.ko: 기계를 작동시키려면 버튼을 눌러.

press.cue: 뉴스를 취재하고 보도하는 언론 매체 전체
press.en: newspapers and journalists considered as a group

ex.en: The scandal was widely reported by the press.
ex.ko: 그 스캔들은 언론에 널리 보도되었다.

previous.en: happening or existing before the one being discussed now

ex.en: I have no previous experience in this field.
ex.ko: 나는 이 분야에서 이전 경험이 없다.

priest.en: a person, especially in the Christian church, who has religious duties and can perform ceremonies

ex.en: The priest led the Sunday service.
ex.ko: 그 신부는 일요일 예배를 이끌었다.

ex.en: The book is primarily aimed at beginners.
ex.ko: 그 책은 주로 초보자를 대상으로 한다.

primary school.en: a school for children between about five and eleven years old

ex.en: My son just started primary school.
ex.ko: 내 아들은 이제 막 초등학교에 입학했다.

primitive.en: belonging to a very early stage of development, before modern technology or culture

ex.en: They lived in primitive conditions without electricity.
ex.ko: 그들은 전기도 없는 원시적인 환경에서 살았다.

principle.en: a basic rule or belief that guides how a person behaves or how something works

ex.en: Honesty is one of her most important principles.
ex.ko: 정직함은 그녀의 가장 중요한 원칙 중 하나이다.

prison.en: a building where criminals are kept as punishment

ex.en: He spent five years in prison.
ex.ko: 그는 5년 동안 감옥에서 지냈다.

prisoner.en: a person who is kept in prison as a punishment

ex.en: The prisoner was released after ten years.
ex.ko: 그 죄수는 10년 만에 석방되었다.

prisoner.en: a person who has been captured, especially during a war

ex.en: The soldiers took several prisoners of war.
ex.ko: 군인들은 여러 명의 전쟁 포로를 잡았다.

privacy.cue: 남에게 보이거나 방해받지 않는 개인적인 영역
privacy.en: the state of being free from others watching or knowing about your personal life

ex.en: She values her privacy and rarely posts online.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사생활을 중요하게 여겨서 온라인에 거의 게시하지 않는다.

prize.en: something given to the winner of a competition or contest

ex.en: She won first prize in the singing contest.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 노래 대회에서 1등 상을 받았다.

probability.en: how likely it is that something will happen

ex.en: There's a high probability of rain tomorrow.
ex.ko: 내일 비가 올 확률이 높다.

proceed.en: continue doing something, especially after stopping

ex.en: Once everyone had arrived, they proceeded with the meeting.
ex.ko: 모두가 도착하자 그들은 회의를 계속 진행했다.

proceed.en: go forward or move on to the next place or step

ex.en: Please proceed to gate 12 for boarding.
ex.ko: 탑승을 위해 12번 게이트로 나아가 주십시오.

process.en: a series of actions or steps taken to achieve a particular result

ex.en: Learning a language is a slow process.
ex.ko: 언어를 배우는 것은 느린 과정이다.

process.en: treat raw materials or information in a series of steps to prepare or change them

ex.en: The factory processes tomatoes into sauce.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 토마토를 소스로 가공한다.

ex.en: The country proclaimed its independence in 1945.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 1945년에 독립을 선언했다.

procrastination.cue: 해야 할 일을 자꾸 미루는 습관
procrastination.en: the habit of delaying something that should be done

ex.en: His procrastination caused him to miss the deadline.
ex.ko: 그의 미루는 습관 때문에 마감을 놓쳤다.

producer.cue: 영화·방송·음반 제작에 자금을 대고 관리하는 사람
producer.en: a person who manages and finances the making of a film, show, or record

ex.en: The film's producer raised money for the project.
ex.ko: 그 영화의 제작자는 프로젝트를 위한 자금을 모았다.

producer.en: a person, company, or country that makes or grows something

ex.en: This region is a major producer of coffee.
ex.ko: 이 지역은 주요 커피 생산지이다.

productive.en: able to produce a lot of good results in an efficient way

ex.en: I had a very productive day at work.
ex.ko: 나는 오늘 직장에서 매우 생산적인 하루를 보냈다.

profession.en: a job that requires special education or training, such as being a doctor or lawyer

ex.en: Teaching is a respected profession.
ex.ko: 교사는 존경받는 직업이다.

professor.en: a teacher of the highest rank at a college or university

ex.en: She is a professor of biology at the university.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 대학교의 생물학 교수이다.

progress.en: movement toward a goal, or improvement over time

ex.en: She is making good progress in her English studies.
ex.ko: 그녀는 영어 공부에서 좋은 진전을 보이고 있다.

progress.en: improve or develop gradually over time

ex.en: His health has progressed well since the surgery.
ex.ko: 수술 이후 그의 건강은 잘 나아지고 있다.

prohibition.cue: 법·규칙으로 어떤 일을 못 하게 하는 것
prohibition.en: an official rule or law that forbids something

ex.en: There is a prohibition on smoking in restaurants.
ex.ko: 식당에서의 흡연은 금지되어 있다.

ex.en: She is a prominent scientist in her field.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 분야에서 저명한 과학자이다.

prominent.en: very noticeable or easy to see

ex.en: The building has a prominent position on the hill.
ex.ko: 그 건물은 언덕 위 눈에 띄는 자리에 있다.

promote.en: give someone a higher position or rank at work

ex.en: She was promoted to manager after two years.
ex.ko: 그녀는 2년 후 매니저로 승진했다.

promote.en: advertise something or help it become more successful or popular

ex.en: The singer is promoting her new album on social media.
ex.ko: 그 가수는 소셜 미디어에서 새 앨범을 홍보하고 있다.

promotion.cue: 직장에서 더 높은 자리로 올라가는 것
promotion.en: a move to a higher position or rank at work

ex.en: He got a promotion last month.
ex.ko: 그는 지난달 승진했다.

promotion.en: activity that helps to advertise or sell something

ex.en: The store is running a big promotion this week.
ex.ko: 그 매장은 이번 주에 큰 판촉 행사를 하고 있다.

pronoun.en: a word such as "he", "she", or "it" that is used instead of a noun

ex.en: "They" is a pronoun that can refer to more than one person.
ex.ko: 'they'는 한 명 이상의 사람을 가리킬 수 있는 대명사이다.

proof.en: facts or information that show something is true

ex.en: Do you have any proof that he was there?
ex.ko: 그가 거기 있었다는 증거가 있어?

properly.en: in a correct or suitable way

ex.en: Make sure you close the door properly.
ex.ko: 문을 제대로 닫았는지 확인해.

property.en: land or buildings owned by someone

ex.en: They bought a property near the beach.
ex.ko: 그들은 해변 근처에 부동산을 샀다.

property.en: a quality or characteristic that something has

ex.en: Water has the property of expanding when it freezes.
ex.ko: 물은 얼면 팽창하는 속성이 있다.

proportion.cue: 전체 중 한 부분이 차지하는 비율
proportion.en: the amount or number of something compared to the whole

ex.en: A large proportion of the population lives in cities.
ex.ko: 인구의 큰 비율이 도시에 살고 있다.

proposal.en: a plan or idea that is formally suggested to be considered

ex.en: The committee will review the proposal next week.
ex.ko: 위원회는 다음 주에 그 제안을 검토할 것이다.

proposal.en: an act of asking someone to marry you

ex.en: She accepted his marriage proposal.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그의 청혼을 받아들였다.

propose.en: formally suggest a plan or idea for people to consider

ex.en: She proposed a new schedule for the project.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 프로젝트를 위한 새 일정을 제안했다.

ex.en: He proposed to her on the beach at sunset.
ex.ko: 그는 석양이 지는 해변에서 그녀에게 청혼했다.

prosperity.en: the state of being successful, especially in a financial sense

ex.en: The country enjoyed years of peace and prosperity.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 수년간 평화와 번영을 누렸다.

prosperous.en: successful, especially in a financial way

ex.en: The town became prosperous after the factory opened.
ex.ko: 그 마을은 공장이 문을 연 후 번영하게 되었다.

protect.en: keep someone or something safe from danger, harm, or damage

ex.en: Wear a helmet to protect your head.
ex.ko: 머리를 보호하기 위해 헬멧을 써.

protection.en: the act of keeping someone or something safe from harm or damage

ex.en: Sunscreen gives protection from the sun.
ex.ko: 선크림은 햇빛으로부터 보호해 준다.

protective.en: designed or intended to protect someone or something from harm

ex.en: Workers must wear protective gloves.
ex.ko: 작업자들은 보호 장갑을 착용해야 한다.

protective.en: feeling or showing a strong wish to keep someone safe from harm

ex.en: He is very protective of his younger sister.
ex.ko: 그는 여동생을 매우 보호하려 든다.

protest.en: a strong public statement or action showing that you disagree with something

ex.en: Hundreds of people joined the protest against the new law.
ex.ko: 수백 명의 사람들이 그 새 법에 반대하는 시위에 참여했다.

protest.en: say or show strongly that you disagree with or object to something

ex.en: Workers protested against the pay cuts.
ex.ko: 근로자들은 임금 삭감에 항의했다.
